note: Community registry for AI agent skills. Add yours via PR — see docs/SPEC.md. Entries point at a SKILL.md folder in a public Git repo.

skills.name: algorithmic-art
skills.description: Generate algorithmic / generative art and visual patterns in code.

skills.name: brand-guidelines
skills.description: Apply a brand's guidelines (colors, typography, voice) consistently when producing assets.

skills.name: canvas-design
skills.description: Compose graphics on an HTML canvas — posters, social images, layouts.

skills.name: changelog
skills.description: Generate or update a CHANGELOG.md in Keep a Changelog format from real git history. Use when cutting a release or asked "what changed since…".

skills.name: claude-api
skills.description: Build with the Claude API: models, tool use, streaming, and Anthropic SDK usage.

skills.name: conventional-commits
skills.description: Write precise Conventional Commits messages from the actual staged diff. Use whenever committing code or asked to write/fix a commit message.

skills.name: doc-coauthoring
skills.description: Collaboratively draft and edit long-form documents with the user.

skills.name: docx
skills.description: Create, edit and analyze Word documents with tracked changes, comments and formatting preservation.

skills.name: engram-memory
skills.description: Give the agent durable, local memory with engram — recall past decisions before answering, persist new decisions and preferences. Use when work spans sessions or the user says "remember".

skills.name: frontend-design
skills.description: Design and build polished, modern frontend UI and components.

skills.name: incident-postmortem
skills.description: Write a blameless, actionable incident postmortem from the raw facts. Use after an outage or when writing up "what happened".

skills.name: internal-comms
skills.description: Write clear internal communications — announcements, updates, and team messaging.

skills.name: mcp-builder
skills.description: Scaffold and build Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers that expose tools to agents.

skills.name: pdf
skills.description: Extract text and tables from PDFs, fill forms, merge/split, OCR, and create new PDFs. Use for anything involving a .pdf.

skills.name: pptx
skills.description: Create, edit and analyze PowerPoint presentations: layouts, templates, charts, automated slide generation.

skills.name: pr-description
skills.description: Write a reviewer-first pull-request description from the actual branch diff. Use when opening a PR or asked to write/improve a PR description.

skills.name: repo-onboarding
skills.description: Systematically map an unfamiliar codebase before changing it — entry points, build/test loop, conventions, data flow. Use when starting work in a repo you haven't seen.

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.description: Author new, well-structured agent skills — scaffolding, conventions and best practices for writing SKILL.md.

skills.name: slack-gif-creator
skills.description: Create animated GIFs sized and optimized for Slack.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.description: Root-cause a bug with a hypothesis-driven loop instead of shotgun edits. Use when a bug isn't obvious after the first look.

skills.name: test-writing
skills.description: Write tests that catch real regressions — behavior-focused, minimal mocking, edge cases from the code's actual branches. Use when adding or backfilling tests.

skills.name: theme-factory
skills.description: Generate cohesive visual themes — color systems, type scales and design tokens.

skills.name: tracelet-instrument
skills.description: Instrument an AI agent or LLM app with OpenTelemetry and watch its runs live in tracelet. Use when adding tracing/observability to an agent or debugging what an agent actually did.

skills.name: web-artifacts-builder
skills.description: Create elaborate, multi-component HTML artifacts with modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind).

skills.name: webapp-testing
skills.description: Interact with and test local web applications using Playwright — verify frontend functionality end to end.

skills.name: xlsx
skills.description: Open, read, edit, format, chart and create spreadsheets (.xlsx/.xlsm/.csv/.tsv), including formulas and data cleaning.
